title: Numbers & Time
shortSummary: Count, tell the time, and handle dates and prices, the numbers you use every day.
culturalNote: French counting has famous quirks above sixty: 70 is soixante-dix (sixty-ten), 80 is quatre-vingts (four-twenties), and 90 is quatre-vingt-dix (four-twenty-ten). The 24-hour clock is standard for schedules and appointments, so 8pm is vingt heures. France also uses a comma for decimals and a space for thousands, and prices are in euros.

grammar.explanation: Above 69, French builds on twenties: 70 is soixante-dix (60+10), 80 is quatre-vingts (4x20), 90 is quatre-vingt-dix (4x20+10). So 75 is soixante-quinze and 95 is quatre-vingt-quinze.
grammar.commonMistake: Inventing septante or huitante. Standard French uses soixante-dix and quatre-vingts (though Belgium and Switzerland differ).

grammar.explanation: Use Il est plus the number plus heures: Il est deux heures (it's two o'clock). Add et demie for half past, et quart for quarter past, moins le quart for quarter to.

examples.native: Il est sept heures et quart.

grammar.commonMistake: Dropping heures: it is deux heures, not just deux for a clock time.

dialogue.setup: Léa calls the cinema to ask about a film and ticket prices.

lines.speaker: Léa
lines.native: Bonjour, à quelle heure commence le film ?
lines.english: Hello, what time does the film start?

lines.speaker: Caissier
lines.native: Il commence à vingt heures trente.

lines.speaker: Léa
lines.native: Je voudrais deux places, s'il vous plaît.
lines.english: I would like two tickets, please.

lines.speaker: Léa
lines.english: Thank you very much. Have a good evening!

quiz.question: How do you say 'eighty' in French?
Quelle _____ est-il ? (asking the time)
quiz.explanation: Quelle heure est-il? means 'what time is it?'.

quiz.question: Which phrase asks the price?
quiz.explanation: Combien ça coûte? means 'how much does it cost?'.

quiz.question: Arrange: heures / trois / il / est
quiz.explanation: Il est (it is) plus the hour: trois heures.
